title: MATRIYA Scientific Task Contract
description: STABLE v1.0. Where the Engine Contract says HOW an engine looks, the Task Contract says WHAT the user is trying to achieve. A user goal (intent) resolves to a task type and a capability graph expressed purely in Capability Ontology terms. GOVERNING RULE: a task NEVER names an engine — only capabilities. The Capability Planner matches the graph to engine capability vectors. Documentation only; no runtime behaviour. Hierarchy: User Goal -> Scientific Task -> Capability Planner -> Engine Contract -> Engine -> Result.

intent.description: The user goal in their own words — an intention, not an API call, e.g. 'reduce water absorption of the plaster'.

valueWeights.description: Optional goal weighting (the Value Field): e.g. { fire: 0.5, cost: 0.3, voc: 0.2 }. Shapes 'recommend'/'optimize'.

capabilityGraph.description: The capabilities this task consumes, as a small DAG. Nodes reference Capability Ontology ids (primitive OR derived) — never engines.

capability.description: A Capability Ontology id: 7 primitives + derived {compare, diagnose, optimize, prioritize}.

minStrength.description: Minimum engine self-rating required to satisfy this node.

outputExpectation.description: What a good result IS. Default 'scientific_narrative' — an explainable, provenance-bound answer, not raw data.

referencesEngines.description: Structural assertion of the governing rule: a task declares it names NO engines. MUST be false.

allOf.$comment: A task that requires generation (directly or via optimize) inherits the engine safety envelope: its generated outputs are hypotheses and promotion is human-gated.
